name: WeCom (企业微信) Bot MCP Server
what_it_does: WeCom (企业微信/WeChat Work) Bot MCP server enabling AI agents to send messages to WeCom enterprise messaging platform — posting text, markdown, images, and card messages to WeCom groups and channels, and integrating China's leading enterprise communication platform into agent-driven business notification and workflow automation.
use_cases: Sending automated alerts and notifications to WeCom groups from monitoring agents
Posting business reports and summaries to WeCom channels from reporting agents
Notifying teams of events and system changes from DevOps agents
Sending approval requests and updates from workflow automation agents
Distributing AI-generated insights to WeCom workspaces from analytics agents
Building WeCom-integrated chatbots from customer service agents
not_for: Teams using Slack, Teams, or Discord (use those specific MCPs)
Personal WeChat messaging (WeCom is enterprise; different app from WeChat)
Reading messages or managing users (bot API is send-only)
best_when: An agent needs to send messages or notifications to WeCom groups — for Chinese enterprise teams using WeCom as their primary communication platform.
avoid_when: Your team uses Slack, Microsoft Teams, or Discord — or you need to read messages rather than just send them.

auth.notes: WeCom Bot webhook URL (contains bot key) required. Create a WeCom group bot in enterprise WeCom admin console. Webhook URL is the authentication mechanism.

pricing.free_tier_limits: WeCom Bot API: free for WeCom enterprise users
pricing.paid_tiers: WeCom: free for SMBs; enterprise features in paid tiers
pricing.notes: WeCom Bot API is free for WeCom users. WeCom itself is free for basic enterprise use. MCP server is free open source.

known_agent_gotchas: WeCom Bot webhook URL contains the authentication token — treat as secret
Bot API is send-only — cannot read messages or manage WeCom resources
Rate limit: 20 messages/minute per bot — implement backoff for bulk notifications
WeCom requires Chinese enterprise verification for domain-based features
Data goes through Tencent/WeCom servers in China — data sovereignty consideration
Markdown support in WeCom is WeCom-specific format — not standard markdown
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Webhook URL contains bot key — store as environment variable. Data processed by Tencent servers in China. Community MCP. Domain verification required for WeCom account.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. Webhook URL is secret — store securely. Data in China (Tencent). Community MCP.
